title: ilex teratopis
intro: Ilex guayusa, also known as Ilex teratopis, is a species of holly tree native to the Amazon rainforest and other parts of South America. It is a rich source of caffeine and other bioactive compounds, earning it the nickname "the Amazonian coffee". The leaves of Ilex teratopis have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Ilex teratopis is often used to support cardiovascular health, improve cognitive function, and boost energy levels, and is typically consumed as a tea or infused in hot water.
